Altered or loss of cortico-medullary differentiation on ultrasound
Raised cortical echo-texture of the kidney on ultrasound
treatments: Drugs as per patient requirement and status (see Table 22.2)

Tarpana with processed milk (Vataja: Panchamula, Jivaniya, Kukkuta flesh; Pittaja: Shankha, Shukti, Madhu, Draksha, Yashti, Kataka; Kaphaja: goat’s liver, Agaru, Priyangu, Devadaru)

Ashchyotana (Vataja: Eranda leaf/root/bark processed goat milk; Pittaja: Haridra, Devadaru, Saindhava processed goat milk; Kaphaja: Lodhra, Yastimadhu, ghee processed goat milk; Raktaja: Sunthi, Triphala, Nimbi, Vasa, Lodhra decoction)
Dhoopana blood-letting followed by Virechana (for Kaphaja Adhimantha)
Leeches applied around the eye to induce blood-letting (for Raktaja Adhimantha)
Dhoomapana with Agaru, Guggulu and Ghrita etc. (Snaihika)
Dhoomapana with Trikatu, Haridra, Sarshapa, Nimba, Guggulu etc. (Kaphahara Dravya)
diet: Spicy and sour food to avoid
Masha soup (soup of black beans) to avoid
lifestyle: Regular mouth washes with normal water or lukewarm water or camphor water
source_references: Sushruta Samhita Dalhana Comm. – Nibandha sangraha, Uttaratantra 6/12-13

symptoms: Pus discharge from gums (Pooyasrava from Dantamansa)

Shri Dalhana Acharya, Sushruta, Sushruta Samhita with Nibandhasamgraha commentary Uttaratantra 11/3-5

treatments: Shirodhara (with lukewarm cow milk for Vata, cold milk/sugarcane juice/Madhu Jala/Mastu for Pitta)
Marsha Nasya (with Bala Taila/Rasnadi Taila for Vata, Pitta pacifying Ghrita for Pitta, Jeevaniya Ghrita for Suryavarta)
Lepa on forehead (with Pittaghna Aushadha Sidhdha Ghrita)
Ghritapana (with cow ghee or Vata pacifying Aushadha Siddha ghee)
